dataset.description: Ground glass opacity and consolidation segmentation from chest CT scans of Long COVID patients
dataset.target_structure: ground glass opacities and consolidations in lungs
dataset.acquisition_info: Siemens scanners, noncontrast chest CT with inspiratory scan at total lung capacity, iterative reconstruction with section thickness × interval of 1 × 0.5 mm
dataset.annotation_info: Manual segmentation by three independent expert annotator pairs (each with a pulmonologist and trainee), blind annotation without automated initialization, final consensus created using STAPLE algorithm
dataset.data_source: Single institution (University of Iowa Hospitals and Clinics) from Long COVID study
dataset.detailed_description: The Long COVID Iowa-UNICAMP (LongCIU) dataset comprises 90 axial CT slices from 9 Long COVID patients with persistent lung opacities. This dataset focuses on the challenging segmentation of ground glass opacities (GGO) and consolidations in the lungs, which are common findings in post-COVID pulmonary sequelae. All images were acquired using Siemens CT scanners with standardized noncontrast protocols and iterative reconstruction. The dataset includes individual annotations from three independent expert annotator pairs plus a STAPLE consensus segmentation, providing valuable data for studying interrater uncertainty in opacity segmentation.
dataset.clinical_relevance: Accurate segmentation of GGO and consolidations is crucial for quantifying lung involvement in Long COVID patients, assessing disease severity, and monitoring treatment response over time.
